exercises.exerciseTypeName: A: Read the passage and write one word in each gap
exercises.title: A: Read the passage and write one word in each gap
exercises.instructions: Read the passage about a mountaineering expedition and write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Read the passage about a mountaineering expedition and write one word in each gap.
exercises.passage:
When Alex signed up for the charity mountain expedition, he was full of enthusiasm. He was slow to catch (1) ...... that climbing a high peak was not simply a matter of putting on a pair of boots and heading uphill. He struggled to keep (2) ...... with the experienced climbers in his team during training, and he had barely studied (3) ...... his navigation skills. He had assumed that his general fitness would allow him to sail (4) ...... the early training sessions without much effort.

On the first morning in the mountains, as the group set off in the cold grey dawn, it suddenly (5) ...... his mind that he might have made a terrible mistake. He questioned the point (6) ...... continuing when every muscle in his body was already aching. After thinking it (7) ...... carefully, he (8) ...... his mind up to keep going rather than turn back. His guide (9) ...... no idea that Alex was doubting himself so deeply.

Then something unexpected happened. While resting on a rocky ledge, Alex looked out across the valley and felt a wave of calm wash over him. It dawned (10) ...... him that the mountain was not his enemy — it was simply asking him to slow down and pay attention. He decided to get (11) ...... with the climb at his own pace. He knew he might not succeed (12) ...... reaching the summit that day, but he pressed on regardless.

By late afternoon, Alex stepped onto the summit and stared at the endless horizon. All at once, everything (13) ...... sense — the hard training, the early starts, the aching legs. He had (14) ...... an important lesson: that with patience and determination, a person can be capable (15) ...... far more than they ever imagined.
explanations: Keep pace → keep **up** with the experienced climbers.
Study purpose → studied **for** his navigation skills.
Complete easily → sail **through** the training sessions.
Had no idea → guide **had** no idea.
Past participle → had **learned** an important lesson.
Capable **of** far more than they imagined.

exercises.exerciseTypeName: B: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals
exercises.title: B: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals
exercises.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals when this is necessary.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals when this is necessary.

items.sentence: She completed the first aid course last month and is still waiting for her (CERTIFY) ...... to arrive.
items.explanation: Noun from CERTIFY → waiting for her **certificate**.

items.sentence: I need to do a lot more (REVISE) ...... before the geography test on Friday.
items.explanation: Noun from REVISE → do more **revision** before the test.

items.sentence: The guide asked everyone to pay (ATTEND) ...... as she explained the safety procedures for the hike.
items.explanation: Fixed phrase → pay **attention**.

items.sentence: The engineer worked through the night but still couldn't find a (SOLVE) ...... to the technical problem.
items.explanation: Noun from SOLVE → find a **solution** to the problem.

items.sentence: The airline warned passengers that unacceptable (BEHAVE) ...... on board would not be tolerated.
items.explanation: Noun from BEHAVE → unacceptable **behaviour** on board.

items.sentence: The coach was delighted to see such a clear (IMPROVE) ...... in the athlete's performance over the season.
items.explanation: Noun from IMPROVE → clear **improvement** in performance.

items.sentence: The travel writer had studied English (LITERATE) ...... before beginning her career in journalism.
items.explanation: Subject noun → studied English **literature**.

exercises.exerciseTypeName: C: Complete the second sentence using the word given
exercises.title: C: Complete the second sentence using the word given
exercises.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
The tour guide made the group wait at the border crossing. **made**
The group ...... at the border crossing by the tour guide.
items.explanation: Passive causative → group **was made to wait**.

items.sentence:
Please don't take photographs inside the ancient temple. **rather**
I ...... take photographs inside the ancient temple.
items.explanation: Preference → I **would rather you did not** take photographs.

items.sentence:
I couldn't understand what the expedition leader was trying to say, so I asked him to repeat it. **getting**
I couldn't understand what the expedition leader ......, so I asked him to repeat it.
items.explanation: Imply/mean → what the leader **was getting at**.

items.sentence:
Counting every kilometre on a long hike is a complete waste of energy. **point**
There ...... every kilometre on a long hike.
items.explanation: Fixed phrase → there **is no point in counting**.

items.sentence:
She just can't decide whether to take the job abroad or stay at home. **minds**
She's ...... whether to take the job abroad or stay at home.
items.explanation: Undecided idiom → **in two minds about** the job.

items.sentence:
My colleague has decided she isn't going to continue with the training programme. **drop**
My colleague has decided ...... the training programme.
items.explanation: Leave a programme → decided **to drop out of** training.

items.sentence:
All the coaches agree that the young athlete is able to do far better. **capable**
All the coaches agree that the young athlete ...... far better.
items.explanation: Ability phrase → **is capable of doing** far better.

items.sentence:
She was delighted that she managed to pass the driving test on her first attempt. **succeeded**
She was delighted that she ...... the driving test on her first attempt.
items.explanation: Succeed + gerund → **succeeded in passing** the test.

items.sentenceBefore: Nobody expected the new project manager
items.sentenceAfter: such a detailed report on her very first day.
items.explanation: Expect + infinitive → expected her **to give** a report.

items.sentenceAfter: abroad on a family holiday for the very first time?
items.explanation: Remember + gerund (past memory) → remember **going** abroad.

items.sentenceBefore: I was reading on the train but I stopped
items.sentenceAfter: the stunning sunset over the mountains.
items.explanation: Purpose → stopped **to watch** the sunset.

items.sentenceAfter: your expense reports before the end of the month.
items.explanation: Forget + infinitive → don't forget **to hand in** reports.

items.sentenceBefore: The customs officer made all the travellers
items.sentenceAfter: while she carefully checked their documents.
items.explanation: Make + bare infinitive → made travellers **stay**.

items.sentenceBefore: 'Did the tour operator know about the safety issue?' 'Well, he denied
items.sentenceAfter: aware of any risk before the trip departed.'
items.explanation: Deny + gerund → denied **being** aware of any risk.

items.sentenceBefore: After years of dedication to the school community, she was thrilled to be appointed as a
items.explanation: Student leader role → appointed as a **prefect**.

items.sentenceBefore: With a bit more practice and determination, I'm sure the team will
items.explanation: Collocation → **achieve** great things.

items.sentenceAfter: you how to navigate using the stars during your expedition?
items.explanation: Instruct someone → Who **taught** you how to navigate?

items.sentenceBefore: The intern didn't perform well during the presentation because she simply hadn't
items.explanation: Prepare for presentation → hadn't **studied**.

items.sentenceBefore: The report was difficult to follow because the author hadn't
items.explanation: Present an argument → hadn't **set out** the main argument.

items.sentenceBefore: The new employee sat in on a workplace safety
items.sentenceAfter: during her very first week on the job.
items.explanation: Training session → workplace safety **lesson**.
